FuelEU Calculator — Page Guide
Summary, workflow, section reference and complete function guide
This calculator is designed for FuelEU Maritime compliance assessment. It combines ship details, voyage/fuel data, GHG intensity calculations, compliance balance, penalty exposure, long-term outlook and CII alignment in one professional single-page tool. Complete Part A and Part B, then click Calculate now to refresh all results.
Summary Section
Purpose
Assess FuelEU GHG intensity, target compliance, energy allocation, penalties and CII output from voyage/fuel inputs.
Main Inputs
Ship particulars, reporting period, fuel type, fuel consumption, voyage scope, distance and ice-related data.
Main Outputs
GHG intensity, compliance balance, penalty estimate, energy summary, long-term outlook and CII rating.
Reports
Save XML for backup, import saved cases, use Excel import/export for voyage data and print/save the final report as PDF.
Step-by-Step Workflow
1
Complete Part A ship and reporting data
Enter the ship identity, ship type, company/reporting details, capacity basis and reporting period. These values support the compliance calculation, report output and CII section.
2
Enter or import Part B voyage/fuel data
Add rows manually or use Excel import. Check fuel type, fuel amount, voyage number, scope, distance and ice-related entries before calculating.
3
Correct validation warnings
If mandatory fields are missing, they are highlighted in red. Click the validation message to jump back to the field requiring correction.
4
Calculate and review results
Click Calculate now. Review Part C compliance balance and penalty, Part D outlook and Part E CII Compliance Outlook.
5
Save, export or print
Use XML for case backup, Excel for voyage/fuel data handling and Print / Save PDF for the final professional report.
Top Bar Functions
Back / Home
Back returns to the previous browser page. Home opens the configured root/home page.
Calculate now
Runs the full calculation and refreshes Part C, Part D and Part E outlook.
Load sample case
Loads a sample case to check workflow, tables, cards and charts.
Save XML / Import XML
Save the current case as XML or restore a previously saved XML case.
Print / Save PDF
Opens the browser print dialog and uses the built-in print report layout.
Reset
Clears the current form and returns the calculator to its reset/default state.
Part A · Ship and Reporting Details
Ship details
Enter ship/company identification, ship type, capacity basis and reporting details used throughout the calculation and report.
Reporting period
Use the reporting period inputs consistently so yearly results, CII and PDF output are aligned.
Collapse arrow
Collapse or expand the section without clearing entered values.
Part B · GHG Intensity Calculation by Fuel
Fuel / voyage rows
Enter each fuel/voyage entry with voyage number, fuel type, fuel amount, scope, distance and applicable ice inputs.
Excel Format
Downloads the prepared Excel template for voyage and fuel data entry.
Import Excel
Imports the completed Excel voyage/fuel table into Part B.
Clear / Add Row / Remove
Clear Part B data, add new rows or remove selected rows from the table.
Search and grouping
Use searchable column headers to locate entries. Grouped voyage rows are visually marked for easier checking.
Fuel factors and PoS/PoC
Use the available edit/update controls for fuel pathway, emission factor and fuel-property configuration where applicable.
Validation
Missing mandatory Part B fields are highlighted in red. Error lines can be clicked to jump to the related field.
Part C · Compliance Balance and Penalty
Result cards
Shows GHG intensity, target comparison, compliance balance and penalty estimate after calculation.
Energy summary
Summarises energy and emissions by fuel/category where applicable.
Detailed result popup
Displays the row-level calculation breakdown used for the compliance balance and penalty result.
Part D · Long-Term Compliance Outlook
Milestone outlook
Reviews longer-term compliance trend and milestone-year exposure based on the calculated case.
Penalty projection
Shows projected penalty exposure using the calculated result basis and configured outlook logic.
Chart and table
Use both chart and table views to check year-by-year trend and penalty impact.
Part E - CII Compliance Outlook
CII basis
Uses Part B fuel amount and unique voyage distance with Part A ship data to calculate CII/AER output.
CII result
Shows attained CII/AER, rating band, yearly table and chart outputs where required inputs are available.
Missing data guidance
If distance or capacity data is missing, the page provides guidance and jump-back support to the relevant inputs.
Important Notes
Before closing
Use Save XML before closing or resetting if you need to continue editing the case later.
Before reporting
Review validation messages, detailed results and energy summary before using Print / Save PDF.

Detailed Result

Each Part B row is shown separately. The CII rating is calculated per row using the same-row PATHWAY NAME / CONSUMER, DISTANCE NM and FUEL AMOUNT, with the CII capacity and ship type taken from Part A.
